## Thesis Organization and Chapter Overview

This thesis is structured to provide a comprehensive framework for
understanding reinforcement learning algorithms applied to trading contexts.

Chapter 1 introduces the problem context, defines the research scope, and
presents the methodological framework used throughout the thesis.

Chapter 2 reviews relevant financial and reinforcement learning literature,
including classical asset-pricing models, empirical anomalies, and prior RL
trading studies.

Chapter 3 outlines machine learning foundations for trading applications and
positions reinforcement learning relative to supervised and unsupervised
approaches.

Chapters 4 and 5 present reinforcement learning foundations and the design of
the trading agent, including action space, state representation, reward
formulation, and policy/value components.

Chapter 6 describes implementation details: data preparation, feature pipeline,
environment assumptions, and the TD3 training workflow.

Chapter 7 reports empirical evaluation, statistical validation, and robustness
assessment.

Chapter 8 summarizes findings, discusses limitations and practical implications,
and proposes directions for future research.
